name: Digital Oscilloscope
description: An electronic instrument that displays voltage as a function of time. The most fundamental measurement tool in electronics and experimental physics.
whyUseIt: Oscilloscopes let you see electrical signals in real time — waveforms, pulses, noise, transients. They are essential for debugging circuits, characterising detectors, and measuring time-varying phenomena. Every physics lab has at least one.
fieldPosition: Experimental physics — fundamental measurement instrument in electronics and detector labs
physicistWorkflow: Connect a probe to the circuit under test, set the timebase and voltage scale, trigger on the signal of interest, and capture or measure.
supervisorExpects: Competent use of oscilloscope controls, proper probe compensation, and correct measurement technique.
computationalImpact: Provides direct visual feedback of electrical signals — indispensable for debugging and characterisation.

vocabulary.definition: The horizontal scale — how many seconds per division on screen
The condition that starts a waveform capture — typically a voltage threshold crossing

beginnerMistakes: Not compensating the probe before measurement
Using the wrong input coupling (AC vs DC) for the signal
